title: Cyperus unistamen
taxonomy: Cyperus unistamen is classified under the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is part of the monocot order Poales. The genus Cyperus is a group of plants known for their distinctive sedgelike appearance. Cyperus unistamen, being a member of this genus, is a monocot plant with a unique set of characteristics. The classification of Cyperus unistamen is further supported by its botanical features, such as its leaf and stem structures.

intro: Cyperus unistamen, also known as Single-stamen flatsedge, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The health benefits of Cyperus unistamen include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus unistamen include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyperus unistamen include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
